hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2-unique ECL2 residues (E192, E193) and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The hypothesis is updated to prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) to disrupt off-target interactions, especially with SSTR3 and SSTR5, while preserving the critical pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding (especially to SSTR3 and SSTR5). Focusing mutations on these positions can disrupt conserved pocket interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations aim to disrupt conserved interactions with off-targets (e.g., hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding interactions at SSTR3/SSTR5) while preserving the overall scaffold and pharmacophore.
